parameters.content:
## Contact / feedback → email (Microsoft Graph API)

The Reckons.AI in-app contact + feedback form POSTs here, so a submission arrives as an email. `src/lib/integrations/n8n/contact.ts` does the POST.

**Send is over the Microsoft Graph API (OAuth2), NOT SMTP.** SMTP was tried first and is a dead end on a modern Microsoft 365 tenant: Security Defaults block Basic-Auth SMTP AUTH (`535 5.7.139 ... locked by your organization's security defaults policy`). Graph over HTTPS works fine *under* Security Defaults, so it is the path that does not rot.

**Setup:**
1. Import this workflow, open the Webhook node, copy the **production** URL. Its path must be `reckons-contact` so it matches `POST {VITE_FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_URL}` — see .env.example. NB the app posts to a PRODUCT endpoint, not the user's own n8n (see contact.ts).
2. Open the **Send Feedback Email** node → create a **Microsoft Outlook OAuth2** credential. In Entra: register an app (**multi-tenant** — n8n uses the `/common` authority), redirect URI `https://<your-n8n>/rest/oauth2-credential/callback`, delegated Graph permission **Mail.Send** + **offline_access**, a client secret (copy the **Value**, not the ID). Paste Client ID + Secret (+ Tenant ID) into n8n and Connect.
3. Change the recipient (`toRecipients`) if it should not be matthew.roe@data-insight.solutions.

**Reply-To is the submitter**, so replying to the notification reaches the person who wrote in.

Payload: `{ name, email, message, source?, submittedAt }`.

**THIS WEBHOOK IS PUBLIC AND UNAUTHENTICATED.** Anyone who learns the URL can post to it. The Validate node drops empty and oversized messages and keeps CR/LF out of mail headers, but it is not a spam filter and nothing rate-limits it. If abused, put n8n behind Cloudflare/rate-limiting or add a shared secret header — the app does not send one today.

nodes.name: Reckons Contact Webhook
nodes.notes: responseMode onReceived returns 200 immediately, which is what makes the form show '✓ Sent' — the app treats any non-2xx as a failure and tells the user to email instead.

nodes.name: Send Feedback Email
nodes.notes: Microsoft Graph API (OAuth2), NOT SMTP — see the About note. Attach a Microsoft Outlook OAuth2 credential. Reply-To is the submitter's address. Swap for the Gmail / Slack / ticket node to change channel — the app does not care how the feedback reaches you.
